name: Leader
effectSummary: Nearby warriors may use this model's Leadership for Leadership tests within the listed range.
pageRef: Core p. 46 and individual warband entries
restrictions: Only the model identified as the warband leader grants this rule.

name: Burn the Witch!
effectSummary: The warrior hates models that can cast spells.
appliesTo: Witch Hunter Captain and Witch Hunters.
restrictions: Applies as listed in the Witch Hunters warband entry.

name: Prayers of Sigmar
effectSummary: The model may use Prayers of Sigmar from the magic/prayers rules.
pageRef: Magic section and individual warband entries
restrictions: Models with this rule use Prayers of Sigmar rather than ordinary spell lists.

name: The Hammer of Sigmar
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 prayer. The caster gains +2 Strength in hand-to-hand combat and wounds caused by the caster deal double damage while maintained.
restrictions: Warrior-Priests and Sigmarite Matriarchs with Prayers of Sigmar.

name: Hearts of Steel
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 prayer. Nearby allies become immune to Fear and All Alone tests, and the warband gains a small Rout test bonus while the prayer remains active.
restrictions: Warrior-Priests and Sigmarite Matriarchs with Prayers of Sigmar.

name: Soulfire
effectSummary: Difficulty 9 prayer. Enemies close to the caster suffer a Strength hit with no armour save; Undead and Possessed are hit harder.
restrictions: Warrior-Priests and Sigmarite Matriarchs with Prayers of Sigmar.

name: Shield of Faith
effectSummary: Difficulty 6 prayer. The caster is immune to spells until the shield fades on a later recovery roll.
restrictions: Warrior-Priests and Sigmarite Matriarchs with Prayers of Sigmar.

name: Healing Hand
effectSummary: Difficulty 5 prayer. Heals one nearby friendly model to full Wounds and helps nearby knocked down or stunned allies recover.
restrictions: Warrior-Priests and Sigmarite Matriarchs with Prayers of Sigmar.

name: Armour of Righteousness
effectSummary: Difficulty 9 prayer. The caster gains a 2+ armour save, causes Fear and is immune to Fear until the listed phase.
restrictions: Warrior-Priests and Sigmarite Matriarchs with Prayers of Sigmar.

name: Animals
effectSummary: Animal warriors do not gain experience.
appliesTo: Warhounds and other animal fighter types.
restrictions: Applies to fighter types marked as animals.

name: Fanatical
effectSummary: The fighter automatically passes Leadership-based tests and may never become warband leader.
restrictions: Applies to Flagellants.

name: Reikland Discipline
effectSummary: Reikland warriors may use their Captain's Leadership within 12 inches instead of the usual 6 inches.
restrictions: Applies only while the Reikland Captain is active and in range.

name: Reikland Marksmen
effectSummary: All Reikland Marksmen add +1 to their Ballistic Skill when recruited.
restrictions: Applies to Marksmen recruited for a Reikland Mercenary warband.

name: Middenheim Physical Prowess
effectSummary: Middenheim Captains and Champions start with Strength 4 instead of Strength 3.
restrictions: Applies only to the listed hero types in a Middenheim warband.

name: Marienburg Traders
effectSummary: Marienburg warbands receive +1 when attempting to find rare items.
restrictions: Applies during trading post rare item searches.

name: Marienburg Wealth
effectSummary: Marienburg warbands start a campaign with 600 gc instead of 500 gc.
restrictions: Campaign starting treasury only; one-off games use the scenario's percentage guidance.

name: Expert Swordsmen
effectSummary: When charging and armed with normal swords, Swordsmen may re-roll failed hit rolls.
restrictions: Only applies when armed with normal swords, not double-handed swords or other weapons.

name: Blessed Sight
effectSummary: The Augur may re-roll failed characteristic tests and failed rolls to hit, and may contribute an extra exploration die if not taken out of action.
restrictions: You must accept the second result for re-rolls.

name: Concussion
effectSummary: The Sigmarite warhammer is especially good at stunning enemies on injury rolls.
restrictions: Applies when using a Sigmarite warhammer in close combat.

name: Holy Weapon
effectSummary: The Sigmarite warhammer gains a to-wound bonus against Possessed or Undead models.
restrictions: Only Matriarchs and Sister Superiors may carry two Sigmarite warhammers.

name: Cannot Be Parried
effectSummary: The steel whip prevents parries from swords or bucklers against its attacks.
restrictions: Applies to attacks made with a steel whip.

name: Whipcrack
effectSummary: The steel whip can grant a bonus attack when charging or when charged, subject to the weapon's restrictions.
restrictions: Only the first whip gains the whipcrack bonus when using two whips.

name: Spellcaster
effectSummary: This model can use a listed spell or ritual list and is eligible for spellcaster-only rules.
pageRef: Core magic rules and individual warband entries
restrictions: Use only where the fighter entry grants access to magic, prayers or rituals.

name: Waaagh! Magic
effectSummary: The Orc Shaman uses the Waaagh! Magic spell list.
restrictions: Orc Shaman only.

name: Led'z Go
effectSummary: Difficulty 9 spell. Orcs or Goblins within 4 inches of the Shaman strike first in hand-to-hand combat until the caster is knocked down, stunned or taken out of action.
restrictions: Orc Shaman with Waaagh! Magic.

name: Oi! Gerroff!
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. Move one enemy within 8 inches D6 inches directly away from the Shaman; collisions cause Strength 3 hits. It cannot target models in hand-to-hand combat.
restrictions: Orc Shaman with Waaagh! Magic.

name: Zzap!
effectSummary: Difficulty 9 spell. The closest enemy target within 12 inches suffers D3 Strength 4 hits with no armour saves.
restrictions: Orc Shaman with Waaagh! Magic.

name: Fooled Ya!
effectSummary: Difficulty 6 spell. No enemy may charge the Shaman during their next turn; if he is in hand-to-hand combat he may immediately move 4 inches away.
restrictions: Orc Shaman with Waaagh! Magic.

name: Clubba
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. The Shaman gains an ectoplasmic club that counts as a normal club with +2 Strength and +1 Attack until he suffers a wound.
restrictions: Orc Shaman with Waaagh! Magic.

name: Fire of Gork
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. Two bolts with 12 inch range each cause D3 Strength 3 hits; they may both hit the closest enemy or split between the two closest enemies.
restrictions: Orc Shaman with Waaagh! Magic.

name: Necromancy
effectSummary: The Necromancer uses the Necromancy spell list and may learn spells as described by the core magic rules.
restrictions: Necromancers and models explicitly granted Necromancy access.

name: Lifestealer
effectSummary: Difficulty 10 spell. Choose one model within 6 inches; it suffers one wound with no save, and the Necromancer gains one Wound for the battle. It does not affect Possessed or Undead models.
restrictions: Necromancer with Necromancy.

name: Re-Animation
effectSummary: Difficulty 5 spell. One Zombie taken out of action in the previous hand-to-hand combat or Shooting phase returns within 6 inches of the Necromancer, but not directly into hand-to-hand combat.
restrictions: Necromancer with Necromancy.

name: Death Vision
effectSummary: Difficulty 6 spell. The Necromancer causes Fear for the duration of the battle.
restrictions: Necromancer with Necromancy.

name: Spell of Doom
effectSummary: Difficulty 9 spell. Choose one enemy within 12 inches; it must roll equal to or under its Strength on a D6 or suffer an Injury table roll.
restrictions: Necromancer with Necromancy.

name: Call of Vanhel
effectSummary: Difficulty 6 spell. One Zombie or Dire Wolf within 6 inches may immediately move up to its Movement distance; if it reaches an enemy, it counts as charging.
restrictions: Necromancer with Necromancy.

name: Spell of Awakening
effectSummary: Automatic spell. If an enemy Hero dies after battle, the Necromancer may raise him as a Zombie retaining profile, weapons and armour, but not equipment, skills, running or future experience.
restrictions: Necromancer with Necromancy; resolved after battle when the enemy Hero is killed.

name: Rituals of Nurgle
effectSummary: The Carnival Master uses the Rituals of Nurgle spell list.
restrictions: Carnival Master only.

name: Daemonic Vigour
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 ritual. Nearby Plague Bearers and Nurglings improve their Daemonic Aura save until the beginning of their next turn.
restrictions: Carnival Master with Rituals of Nurgle.

name: Buboes
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 ritual. A single enemy in range must pass a Toughness test or lose a Wound with no armour save.
restrictions: Carnival Master with Rituals of Nurgle.

name: Stench of Nurgle
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 ritual. Living creatures in range must pass a Toughness test or lose an Attack until their next turn.
restrictions: Carnival Master with Rituals of Nurgle.

name: Pestilence
effectSummary: Difficulty 10 ritual. Enemy models within range suffer a Strength 3 hit with no armour save.
restrictions: Carnival Master with Rituals of Nurgle.

name: Scabrous Hide
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 ritual. The Carnival Master gains a 2+ armour save that replaces any normal armour save until the listed phase.
restrictions: Carnival Master with Rituals of Nurgle.

name: Nurgle's Rot
effectSummary: Difficulty 9 ritual. Enemy models in base contact with the Carnival Master test Toughness or contract Nurgle's Rot.
restrictions: Carnival Master with Rituals of Nurgle.

name: Dangerous to Know
effectSummary: Hired swords never join the Carnival of Chaos.
restrictions: Use when hired swords are implemented for roster hiring.

name: Unnatural Strength
effectSummary: The Brute starts with the Strongman skill.
restrictions: Applies to Brutes.

name: Tainted
effectSummary: A Tainted One must begin with at least one Blessing of Nurgle; further Blessings are paid at the higher listed multiplier.
pageRef: Empire in Flames p. 63 and p. 66
restrictions: Tainted Ones only.

name: Blessings of Nurgle
effectSummary: Paid Tainted One upgrades selected from the Blessings list; the app tracks required selection and doubled cost after the first Blessing.
appliesTo: Tainted Ones and Blessing option records.
restrictions: Tainted Ones only.

name: Fear
effectSummary: Enemies must pass a Fear test to charge or fight the model as described in the core rules.
appliesTo: Models and effects that cause Fear.
restrictions: Use only when a fighter or effect grants Fear.

name: Animosity
effectSummary: At the start of the Orc player's turn, Orc and Goblin henchmen that are not in hand-to-hand combat test for squabbling. On a failed test they may fight, shoot at or insult allies, or rush toward the enemy.
restrictions: Do not roll for models already engaged in hand-to-hand combat.

name: Distasteful Company
effectSummary: Orc warbands may only hire Pit Fighters, Ogre Bodyguards or Warlocks as Hired Swords.
restrictions: Use when hiring Hired Swords.

name: Not Orcs
effectSummary: Goblin Warriors and Cave Squigs taken out of action count as half a model for Orc Mob Rout test thresholds.
restrictions: Applies to Orc Mob Rout test calculations.

name: Runts
effectSummary: Goblin Warriors may gain experience, but if they roll 'That Lad's Got Talent' they are removed instead of becoming heroes.
restrictions: Goblin Warriors only.

name: Movement
effectSummary: Cave Squigs move 2D6 inches instead of using a fixed Movement value. They never run or declare charges, but count as charging if their normal movement contacts an enemy.
restrictions: Cave Squigs only.

name: Minderz
effectSummary: Each Cave Squig must stay near a Goblin Warrior. If it starts movement without a nearby Goblin, it goes wild and moves randomly for the rest of the game.
restrictions: Squig Prodders extend the listed control range.

name: Animals
effectSummary: Cave Squigs do not gain experience.
restrictions: Cave Squigs only.

name: Squig Prodder
effectSummary: A Goblin with a Squig Prodder keeps Cave Squigs in line at the larger listed range; in combat the prodder is treated like a spear.
restrictions: Goblins only.

name: Mad Cap Mushrooms
effectSummary: A Goblin may take Mad Cap Mushrooms at the start of any turn. They are required to use a Ball and Chain.
appliesTo: Mad Cap Mushrooms and Ball and Chain.
restrictions: Common for Orc warbands that include Goblins.

name: Incredible Force
effectSummary: Ball and Chain wounds allow no armour saves and each successful wound causes D3 wounds.
restrictions: Use only for Ball and Chain attacks.

name: Random
effectSummary: A Ball and Chain wielder moves 2D6 inches using the listed random movement process, counts as charging when contacting a model, and is taken out of action if he hits a solid obstruction.
restrictions: Use while the Ball and Chain is being swung.

name: Cumbersome
effectSummary: A model with a Ball and Chain may carry no other weapons or equipment, except the Mad Cap Mushrooms required to use it.
restrictions: Requires Mad Cap Mushrooms.

name: Unwieldy
effectSummary: At the end of the battle, a model that used a Ball and Chain must roll for Injury as if taken out of action, unless it already rolled for being taken out of action.
restrictions: Use after battles where the Ball and Chain was used.

name: Regeneration
effectSummary: When an enemy wounds the Troll, roll a D6; on 4+ the wound is ignored. Fire and fire-based magic cannot be regenerated, and Trolls do not roll for post-battle Injury.
restrictions: Troll only.

name: Dumb Monster
effectSummary: The Troll never gains experience or new skills.
restrictions: Troll only.

name: Always Hungry
effectSummary: After each game, the warband must pay 15 gc upkeep or sacrifice two Goblin Warriors or Cave Squigs to keep the Troll; otherwise it leaves.
restrictions: Troll only; resolve after each game.

name: Vomit Attack
effectSummary: Instead of normal attacks, the Troll may make one automatic Strength 5 hit in hand-to-hand combat that ignores armour saves.
restrictions: Use instead of the Troll's normal attacks.

name: Immune to Psychology
effectSummary: The model is not affected by psychology such as Fear and never leaves combat because of psychology.
restrictions: Applies only to the listed Undead fighter entries.

name: Immune to Poison
effectSummary: The model is not affected by poison.
restrictions: Applies only where the fighter entry grants this rule.

name: No Pain
effectSummary: Treat a stunned result on the Injury chart as knocked down instead.
restrictions: Applies only where the fighter entry grants this rule.

name: May Not Run
effectSummary: The model may not run, but may charge normally.
restrictions: Applies only where the fighter entry grants this rule.

name: No Brain
effectSummary: The model never gains experience.
restrictions: Zombies only.

name: Unliving
effectSummary: The model never gains experience.
restrictions: Dire Wolves only.

name: Charge
effectSummary: Dire Wolves fight with 2 Attacks instead of 1 during a turn in which they charge.
restrictions: Dire Wolves only, during the turn they charge.

name: Nurgle's Rot
effectSummary: A contagious disease effect associated with Nurgle; living enemies wounded by the bearer may suffer campaign consequences.
restrictions: Use the source rule for full campaign resolution.

name: Cloud of Flies
effectSummary: Close combat opponents suffer a to-hit penalty against the model.
appliesTo: Plague Bearers, Nurglings and the Cloud of Flies Blessing.
restrictions: Applies only to listed Nurgle models or Blessings.

name: Stream of Corruption
effectSummary: Short-ranged Strength 3 shooting attack that allows no armour save.
appliesTo: Plague Bearers and the Stream of Corruption Blessing.
pageRef: Empire in Flames p. 62 and p. 66
restrictions: Use only when the model entry or Blessing grants it.

name: Daemonic Instability
effectSummary: The model may be banished instead of rolling normal injury, and may be destroyed after a failed rout-related Leadership test.
restrictions: Use the source entry for the exact roll and timing.

name: Daemonic Aura
effectSummary: The model has a special save against wounds, modified by Strength and ignored by magic as described in the source.
restrictions: Use the source entry for the exact save.

name: Swarm
effectSummary: Nurglings may exceed the normal five-model henchman group size while still obeying the warband's overall warrior limit.
restrictions: Nurglings only.

name: Daemon
effectSummary: The model follows the Daemon special rules referenced by its warband entry.
restrictions: Use the source entry for the full Daemon rule text.

name: Plague Cart Warband Size
effectSummary: A Carnival of Chaos warband with a Plague Cart may include two additional warriors.
appliesTo: Carnival of Chaos warbands with a Plague Cart.
pageRef: Empire in Flames p. 60 and p. 64
restrictions: Maximum one Plague Cart.

name: Plague Cart
effectSummary: Warband vehicle entry with special campaign and movement rules. The app models its roster cost, cap and warband-size bonus.
restrictions: Use the source entry for full vehicle handling.

name: Perfect Killer
effectSummary: Enemies suffer an extra -1 armour save modifier against wounds inflicted by the Assassin Adept.
restrictions: Assassin Adept only.

name: Magic of the Horned Rat
effectSummary: The Eshin Sorcerer and Pestilens Sorcerer use the Magic of the Horned Rat spell list and may learn spells as described by the source rules.
restrictions: Eshin Sorcerer or Pestilens Sorcerer only.

name: Warpfire
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. A short-ranged green flame hits the first model in its path with multiple Strength 4 hits and splashes nearby models with Strength 3 hits.
appliesTo: Magic of the Horned Rat spell list.
restrictions: Eshin Sorcerer spell result 1.

name: Children of the Horned Rat
effectSummary: Automatic spell used before the game once per battle. Summons temporary Giant Rats that do not count toward warband maximum size.
appliesTo: Magic of the Horned Rat spell list.
restrictions: Eshin Sorcerer spell result 2.

name: Gnawdoom
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. A single model within short range suffers many low-Strength hits.
appliesTo: Magic of the Horned Rat spell list.
restrictions: Eshin Sorcerer spell result 3.

name: Black Fury
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. The Sorcerer may make an immediate charge within the listed range and gains extra Attacks and Strength for that turn's hand-to-hand phase.
appliesTo: Magic of the Horned Rat spell list.
restrictions: Eshin Sorcerer spell result 4.

name: Eye of the Warp
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. Standing models in base contact test Leadership; failures suffer a Strength 3 hit and flee directly away.
appliesTo: Magic of the Horned Rat spell list.
restrictions: Eshin Sorcerer spell result 5.

name: Sorcerer's Curse
effectSummary: Difficulty 6 spell. A model within range must re-roll successful armour saves and hit rolls for the listed phases.
appliesTo: Magic of the Horned Rat spell list.
restrictions: Eshin Sorcerer spell result 6.

name: Leader
effectSummary: Friendly warriors within 6 inches of the Plague Priest may use his Leadership instead of their own.
restrictions: Skaven of Clan Pestilens leader only.

name: +1 Enemy Armour Save
effectSummary: A model wounded by a Disease Dagger gains +1 to its armour save, or a 6+ armour save if it had none.
restrictions: Use when resolving wounds from a Disease Dagger.

name: Infecting
effectSummary: On a natural 6 to hit, the target must take a Toughness test. If the D6 result is higher than its Toughness, it suffers an additional automatic wound. Undead and Possessed are immune.
restrictions: The extra wound is in addition to the normal dagger hit.

name: Two-handed
effectSummary: A model using a Censer may not use a shield, buckler or additional weapon in hand-to-hand combat. A shield still gives its bonus against shooting.
restrictions: Use when fighting with a Censer.

name: Concussion
effectSummary: When the Censer rolls on the injury table, a 2-4 result counts as Stunned.
restrictions: Use when resolving injuries caused by the Censer.

name: Heavy
effectSummary: The Censer grants +2 Strength in close combat, as shown in its weapon profile.
restrictions: This Strength bonus is limited by the Exhausting rule.

name: Exhausting
effectSummary: The Censer's +2 Strength bonus applies only in the first turn of each hand-to-hand combat.
restrictions: Later rounds use the wielder's normal Strength unless another rule changes it.

name: Fumes
effectSummary: After all attacks are resolved, roll a D6 for every model in base contact with the Censer bearer. On a 1, that model suffers a wound. Plague Monks and Censer Bearers are immune.
restrictions: Resolve after attacks from the combat have been worked out.

name: Pack Size
effectSummary: Any number of Giant Rats may be recruited, subject to the warband's overall warrior limit.
restrictions: Giant Rats only.

name: Animals
effectSummary: Giant Rats are animals and do not gain experience.
restrictions: Giant Rats only.

name: Stupidity
effectSummary: The model is subject to Stupidity tests and the related behavior described in the core rules.
appliesTo: Rat Ogre and other models with Stupidity.
restrictions: Rat Ogres ignore this while a Skaven Hero is within the listed range.

name: Large Target
effectSummary: The model is a large target for shooting and visibility rules.
appliesTo: Rat Ogre and other large models.
restrictions: Applies only to models marked as large targets.

name: Poison
effectSummary: Blowpipe darts can automatically wound on a high To Hit roll, but cannot cause critical hits.
restrictions: Use only when firing a blowpipe.

name: Stealthy
effectSummary: A hidden Skaven can fire a blowpipe without automatically revealing itself; the target may test to spot the shooter.
restrictions: Use only when firing a blowpipe while hidden.

name: Fire Every Other Turn
effectSummary: The warplock pistol follows the fire-every-other-turn restriction.
appliesTo: Warplock pistol and brace of warplock pistols.
restrictions: Skaven-only warplock pistols.

name: Pair
effectSummary: Fighting claws are used as a pair and grant an extra attack.
restrictions: Skaven-only fighting claws.

name: Climb
effectSummary: Fighting claws add to Initiative tests for climbing.
restrictions: Skaven-only fighting claws.

name: Parry
effectSummary: Fighting claws may parry and re-roll a failed parry attempt once.
restrictions: Skaven-only fighting claws.

name: Cumbersome
effectSummary: A model armed with fighting claws may not use any other weapons in the battle.
restrictions: The app blocks combining fighting claws with other weapon items.

name: Pair
effectSummary: Weeping blades are used as a pair and grant an extra attack.
restrictions: Skaven-only weeping blades.

name: Venomous
effectSummary: Weeping blades count as permanently coated in black lotus; no additional poison may be applied.
restrictions: Skaven-only weeping blades.

name: Parry
effectSummary: Weeping blades are swords and can be used for parrying.
restrictions: Skaven-only weeping blades.

name: Hate Dark Elves
effectSummary: All Shadow Warrior warband members, excluding Hired Swords, hate Dark Elves.
restrictions: Does not apply to Hired Swords.

name: Excellent Sight
effectSummary: This warrior spots Hidden enemies at twice the normal distance: twice Initiative in inches.
appliesTo: Elves in a Shadow Warrior warband and Elf Rangers.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior warband members; Hired Swords only if their own entry grants it.

name: Distaste for Poison
effectSummary: Shadow Warrior warband members may not use poisons or drugs of any type.
restrictions: Applies to warriors in the warband, not Hired Swords unless their own rules say otherwise.

name: Unforgiving
effectSummary: In multiplayer games the warband may not ally with warbands of a Chaotic nature.
restrictions: Campaign/social rule; record manually when arranging multiplayer games.

name: Tolerant
effectSummary: Shadow Warriors may hire non-chaotic, non-evil Hired Swords and avoid poison specialists.
restrictions: No evil, Chaotic or poison-specialist Hired Swords.

name: Cannot Cast In Armour
effectSummary: This spellcaster may wear armour if allowed by the equipment list, but cannot cast spells while wearing armour.
restrictions: The app blocks selectable spells while armour is equipped.

name: Shadow Magic
effectSummary: The Shadow Weaver uses the Shadow Magic spell list.
restrictions: Shadow Weaver only.

name: Pool of Shadow
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. The caster and models within 6 inches may Hide as if screened by terrain, even after marching, and count as in cover against shooting; the effect ends if an enemy enters the area and lasts until the caster's next turn.
restrictions: Shadow Weaver with Shadow Magic.

name: The Living Shadows
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. One enemy within 12 inches and within 2 inches of suitable shadowed terrain suffers one Strength 4 hit with no armour save.
restrictions: Shadow Weaver with Shadow Magic.

name: Wings of Night
effectSummary: Difficulty 6 spell. If the caster is within 2 inches of suitable terrain, move him up to 12 inches to another place within 2 inches of suitable terrain; contact with an enemy counts as charging.
restrictions: Shadow Weaver with Shadow Magic.

name: Cloak of Darkness
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. The caster cannot be attacked while he avoids attacking enemy models; the effect ends when he attacks.
restrictions: Shadow Weaver with Shadow Magic.

name: Shadowbind
effectSummary: Difficulty 9 spell. One enemy within 24 inches and within 2 inches of suitable terrain cannot move unless it passes the listed Strength test; if attacked while bound, treat it as Stunned.
restrictions: Lasts until the Shadow Weaver suffers a Wound or attempts another spell.

name: Shield of Shadow
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. The caster or one warband member within 12 inches receives a 5+ armour save that is not modified by Strength until the caster's next turn.
restrictions: Shadow Weaver with Shadow Magic.

name: Save Modifier
effectSummary: An Elf Bow is Range 36 inches, Strength 3, and applies a -1 armour save modifier.
restrictions: Use when resolving Elf Bow shooting.

name: Ithilmar Weapon
effectSummary: An ithilmar close-combat weapon gives the user +1 Initiative in hand-to-hand combat.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior creation cost is modelled as separate 2x-price items.

name: Ithilmar Armour
effectSummary: Provides a 5+ armour save and does not cause the normal movement penalty when used with a shield.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior heroes only at creation.

name: Standard of Nagarythe
effectSummary: Acts as a second rally point within 12 inches. If captured, Shadow Warriors gain Hatred for the rest of the game and may not voluntarily rout.
restrictions: Requires one free hand; may only be bought when creating the warband.

name: War Horn of Nagarythe
effectSummary: Once per game at the start of a Shadow Warrior turn, all warband members gain +1 Leadership, to a maximum of 10, until the next turn.
restrictions: May be used immediately before a Rout test.

name: Elven Cloak
effectSummary: Enemy shooting attacks suffer an additional -1 to hit the wearer.
restrictions: Use while resolving shooting against the wearer.

name: Elven Wine
effectSummary: One-use pre-battle item. The Shadow Warrior warband is immune to Fear for that battle.
restrictions: Shadow Warriors only; one use only.

name: Elven Runestones
effectSummary: A mage may try to dispel a spell cast against himself or another warband member; if the dispel roll succeeds the spell fails.
restrictions: Shadow Weavers only at creation; spell-users only when found later.

name: Scaly Skin
effectSummary: Skinks have a natural 6+ save. Light armour and shields improve this save as listed.
restrictions: This natural save cannot be modified beyond 6+ by Strength modifiers, but no-save injury results still negate it.

name: Scaly Skin
effectSummary: Saurus have a natural 5+ save. Light armour and shields improve this save as listed.
restrictions: This natural save cannot be modified beyond 6+ by Strength modifiers, but no-save injury results still negate it.

name: Scaly Skin
effectSummary: Kroxigor have a natural 4+ save.
restrictions: Kroxigor only.

name: Cold Blooded
effectSummary: Roll 3D6 and use the lowest two dice for psychology tests and Rout tests. Saurus and Kroxigor Leadership cannot be used for Rout tests.
restrictions: Applies to Lizardmen warbands.

name: Bite Attack
effectSummary: Saurus make a bite attack using their own Strength. It has no unarmed penalty and always strikes last, even after double-handed weapons.
restrictions: Saurus only.

name: Aquatic
effectSummary: The model moves through water terrain with no penalty and counts as in cover while in water.
restrictions: Use in scenarios with water terrain.

name: Jungle Born
effectSummary: Skinks move through jungle terrain without penalty.
restrictions: Use in jungle terrain.

name: Saurus Rarity
effectSummary: The warband may not include more Saurus Braves than Skink Braves.
restrictions: The app validates this ratio for Saurus Brave henchmen.

name: Animal
effectSummary: Kroxigor are slow-witted and do not gain experience.
restrictions: Kroxigor only.

name: Lizardmen Magic
effectSummary: The Skink Priest uses Lizardmen Magic. These spells work like Prayers of Sigmar and may be used while wearing armour.
restrictions: Skink Priest only.

name: Chotec's Wrath
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. The closest enemy within 10 inches suffers one Strength 5 hit; against a model wearing armour, add +1 Strength and +1 to the injury roll.
restrictions: Skink Priest with Lizardmen Magic.

name: Sotec's Blessing
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. One Lizardman within 6 inches gains a random blessing: improved Ballistic Skill or close-combat hit roll, +1 Toughness, or +1 Movement and Initiative.
restrictions: Only one model may be affected at a time; ends if the Priest or target is Stunned or Out of Action.

name: Huanchi's Stealth
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. Skinks within 6 inches, including the Priest, may immediately Hide if in cover, even after running or shooting.
restrictions: Affects Skinks within range.

name: The Old Ones' Protection
effectSummary: Difficulty 6 spell. The Priest and Lizardmen within 4 inches gain a 4+ save against spells and prayers until the Priest is Out of Action.
restrictions: Skink Priest with Lizardmen Magic.

name: Tinci's Rage
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. One Lizardman within 6 inches gains Frenzy and +1 Strength; the Priest must pass Leadership at the start of later turns to keep it going.
restrictions: Only one model may be affected at a time; ends if the Priest or target is Stunned or Out of Action.

name: Itzl's Speed
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. One Lizardman within 6 inches may move up to 4 inches as an extra run; it cannot charge and cannot move if it already fired a missile weapon.
restrictions: Skink Priest with Lizardmen Magic.

name: Dangerous
effectSummary: If the To Hit roll with bolas is a natural 1, the wielder takes a Strength 3 hit.
restrictions: Use when firing bolas.

name: Entangle
effectSummary: A model hit by bolas is not wounded, but cannot move and suffers -2 Weapon Skill in hand-to-hand combat until it frees itself on a 4+ in Recovery.
restrictions: The entangled model may still shoot normally.

name: Thrown Weapon
effectSummary: Javelins do not suffer penalties for range or moving.
restrictions: Use when throwing javelins.

name: Blowpipe
effectSummary: Use the core blowpipe weapon rules. The Lizardmen list makes blowpipes available to Skinks.
restrictions: Skinks equipment list.

name: Poisoned Weapons
effectSummary: Skink heroes may buy Dark Venom or Black Lotus as common items, but may only use them on missile weapons.
restrictions: Saurus use Dark Venom or Black Lotus through the normal trading chart instead.

name: Reptile Venom
effectSummary: One-battle Skink henchman poison for missile weapons. It adds +1 Strength but no extra save modifier.
restrictions: Skink henchmen only; group equipment must remain uniform.

name: Oversized Jaws
effectSummary: The Saurus hero's bite attack gains +1 Strength.
restrictions: Saurus heroes only; one Sacred Marking per hero.

name: Poison Glands
effectSummary: The Skink hero may attack with poisonous bites instead of weapons. These bites use the Saurus bite timing and add +1 to injury rolls.
restrictions: Skink heroes only; one Sacred Marking per hero.

name: Mark of the Old Ones
effectSummary: Once per battle, the hero may change one failed roll into a success for an action he is making himself, including a failed Rout test.
appliesTo: Mark of the Old Ones Sacred Marking.
restrictions: Lizardmen heroes only; one Sacred Marking per hero.

name: Frenzy
effectSummary: The model follows the Frenzy psychology rules, including extra ferocity and compulsory behavior as described in the core rules.
appliesTo: Models and effects that grant Frenzy.
restrictions: Use only when a fighter or effect grants Frenzy.

name: Natives
effectSummary: Forest Goblins suffer no movement penalties from moving through wooded terrain.
restrictions: Applies in wooded terrain.

name: Animosity
effectSummary: Forest Goblin henchmen and Braves are subject to the standard Orc and Goblin Animosity rules.
restrictions: Do not roll for models already engaged in hand-to-hand combat.

name: Ride Spiders
effectSummary: The Chieftain can ride Giant Spiders and the Gigantic Spider using the listed mounted rules.
restrictions: Mounted-rider details are handled manually.

name: Forest Goblin Magic
effectSummary: The Forest Goblin Shaman starts with one randomly chosen Forest Goblin spell.
restrictions: Forest Goblin Shaman only.

name: Wind of Gork
effectSummary: Difficulty 6 spell. Range 12 inches; the first model in its path must roll under Toughness or suffer a Strength 2 hit and be knocked down automatically.
restrictions: Forest Goblin Shaman with Forest Goblin Magic.

name: Gaze of Mork
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. Range 12 inches; D3 Strength 3 hits strike the first model in their path.
restrictions: Forest Goblin Shaman with Forest Goblin Magic.

name: 'Eadbanger
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. Range 6 inches; fire bolts equal to the Shaman's Attacks, resolved at Strength equal to his Toughness. After resolving, on a roll of 1 the Shaman goes out of action.
restrictions: Forest Goblin Shaman with Forest Goblin Magic.

name: Leap of Waaagh!
effectSummary: Difficulty 7 spell. The Shaman or a Goblin within 3 inches may move up to 12 inches; if this reaches close combat, the model counts as charging.
restrictions: Forest Goblin Shaman with Forest Goblin Magic.

name: Idol of Gork
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. The Shaman gains +1 WS, +1 Strength and +1 Attack until he takes a wound.
restrictions: Forest Goblin Shaman with Forest Goblin Magic.

name: 'Ere We Go!
effectSummary: Difficulty 8 spell. Friendly models within 6 inches of the Shaman treat stunned injury results as knocked down instead until the Shaman takes a wound.
restrictions: Forest Goblin Shaman with Forest Goblin Magic.

name: Strike First
effectSummary: The Boss Pole acts as a spear and strikes first in the first turn of hand-to-hand combat, even if charged.
appliesTo: Boss Pole and Forest Goblin spears.
restrictions: First turn of hand-to-hand combat.

name: Cavalry Bonus
effectSummary: If mounted optional rules are used, a mounted warrior armed with a spear-like weapon gains +1 Strength on the charge for that turn.
appliesTo: Boss Pole and Forest Goblin spears.
restrictions: Mounted optional rules only.

name: Ignore Animosity
effectSummary: The hero carrying a Boss Pole and Goblin henchmen within 6 inches ignore Animosity.
restrictions: Use while the Boss Pole carrier is active and within range.

name: Poison
effectSummary: A blowpipe automatically wounds on a To Hit roll of 6, cannot cause critical hits and gives the target a +1 armour save modifier.
restrictions: Use when firing a blowpipe.

name: Stealthy
effectSummary: A hidden Goblin may fire a blowpipe without automatically revealing itself; the target may test to spot the shooter.
restrictions: Use when firing a blowpipe while hidden.

name: Poisoned Weapon
effectSummary: The selected weapon adds +1 to injury rolls. Once bought, the poison is fixed to that weapon and may not be traded or sold.
restrictions: Choose the affected weapon manually in notes.

name: Magic Gubbinz
effectSummary: The Shaman may re-roll a failed magic test on a D6 roll of 4+.
restrictions: Forest Goblin Shaman only.

name: Red Toof Tribal Jewellery
effectSummary: The jewellery cannot be removed and makes the model subject to Frenzy.
restrictions: Forest Goblins only.

name: Poisoned Attack
effectSummary: Giant Spider attacks are poisoned and count as Strength 4, but do not modify armour saves.
restrictions: Mounted spider handling remains manual.

name: Wall Walk
effectSummary: Giant Spiders and their riders may move up and down walls without Initiative tests, with the listed limits for jumps and diving charges.
restrictions: Mounted spider handling remains manual.

name: Mounted Warrior
effectSummary: Mounted Heroes are treated as one model with their mount. Use the rider's Leadership, Toughness and Wounds. The model gets a 6+ armour save, or +1 better armour save if the rider wears armour, cannot ride indoors, and loses half Movement when mounting or dismounting.
restrictions: Optional mounted combat rules; Heroes only unless a scenario or house rule says otherwise.

name: Mount Casualty
effectSummary: If a mounted warrior is taken out of action, roll D6 after the battle. On 1-2 the mount is killed and removed from the roster.
appliesTo: Mounted warriors taken out of action.
restrictions: Optional mounted combat rules.

name: Battle Schooled
effectSummary: The rider may re-roll one failed loss of control test while riding this mount.
appliesTo: Warhorses, Elven Steeds and similar trained mounts.
restrictions: Optional Blazing Saddles mounted combat rules.

name: Non-combatant
effectSummary: This mount does not make attacks and may not be used to charge into combat unless another rule says otherwise.
restrictions: Optional mounted combat rules.

name: Ferocious Charge
effectSummary: When the War Boar charges, the boar's own attack is made at +2 Strength. This bonus applies to the boar, not the rider.
restrictions: Optional Blazing Saddles mounted combat rules.

name: Thick Skinned
effectSummary: A War Boar gives the rider an additional +1 armour save bonus, making the total mounted bonus +2.
restrictions: Optional Blazing Saddles mounted combat rules.

name: Scaly
effectSummary: A Cold One gives the rider an additional +1 armour save bonus, making the total mounted bonus +2.
restrictions: Optional Blazing Saddles mounted combat rules.

name: Fear
effectSummary: Enemies must pass a Leadership test to charge this model. If charged by this model or fighting it after failing a Fear test, affected enemies only hit on 6s in that first combat round.
appliesTo: Models and mounts that cause Fear.
restrictions: Use when a model or equipment entry grants Fear.

name: Stupidity
effectSummary: At the start of the model's turn, take a Leadership test. If failed, the model cannot cast spells or fight until its next turn; if not in combat, roll D6: 1-3 moves half speed straight ahead, 4-6 does nothing.
appliesTo: Models and mounts subject to Stupidity.
restrictions: Use when a model or equipment entry grants Stupidity.

name: Berserkers
effectSummary: Red Toof Boyz are subject to Frenzy. If they begin their turn within charge range of an enemy, they are immune to Animosity for that turn.
restrictions: Red Toof Boyz only.

name: Sluggas
effectSummary: In the shooting phase, Sluggas may throw throwing weapons up to three times. This may not be combined with Quick Shot if promoted to hero status.
restrictions: Sluggas with throwing weapons.

name: Poisonous
effectSummary: When the Gigantic Spider wounds an enemy and rolls on the injury table, 1 is knocked down, 2-4 is stunned and 5-6 is out of action.
restrictions: Gigantic Spider only.

name: Native
effectSummary: The Gigantic Spider moves through wooded terrain without penalty.
restrictions: Wooded terrain.

name: Non-sentient
effectSummary: The Gigantic Spider is subject to Stupidity and never gains experience because it is an animal.
restrictions: Gigantic Spider only.

name: May Ride
effectSummary: The Forest Goblin Chieftain may ride the Gigantic Spider, gaining +1 armour save and using the spider's Movement; hit allocation is handled by the listed mounted rules.
appliesTo: Gigantic Spider and Forest Goblin Chieftain.
restrictions: Full mounted-rider handling remains manual.
